title: Data Structures and Algorithms in Python
description: Python developers who need durable algorithmic reasoning, implementation skill, counterexample habits, and evidence-based data-structure selection. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate algorithm contracts, correctness evidence, complexity, and benchmarking through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate linear structures, hash tables, trees, heaps, tries, and graph representations through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate sorting, searching, recursion, traversal, components, cycles, and path reconstruction through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate invariant testing, adversarial inputs, composite design, and stakeholder defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.
misconceptions: An example input fully specifies a problem.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify inputs, outputs, preconditions, postconditions, and invalid cases before selecting an algorithm.

competencies.statement: State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.
misconceptions: Passing tests is the same as proving correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State partial and total correctness claims that can be challenged by counterexamples.

competencies.statement: Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.
misconceptions: Random inputs reliably cover boundary behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct normal, boundary, duplicate, empty, sorted, reversed, and adversarial cases.

competencies.statement: Choose a computational model and counted operation that matches stakeholder cost.
misconceptions: Runtime seconds are the only valid cost model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a computational model and counted operation that matches stakeholder cost.

competencies.statement: Implement a clear correct baseline before optimizing and preserve it as an oracle.
misconceptions: A baseline is wasted work once optimization starts.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement a clear correct baseline before optimizing and preserve it as an oracle.

competencies.statement: Define input size and dominant operations for scalar, sequence, graph, and numeric problems.
misconceptions: Input size always equals one list length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define input size and dominant operations for scalar, sequence, graph, and numeric problems.

competencies.statement: Distinguish upper, tight, and lower asymptotic bounds without dropping relevant variables.
misconceptions: Big O always means exact worst-case runtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish upper, tight, and lower asymptotic bounds without dropping relevant variables.

competencies.statement: Simplify sums, products, and nested-loop costs while preserving independent dimensions.
misconceptions: Every nested loop is quadratic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Simplify sums, products, and nested-loop costs while preserving independent dimensions.

competencies.statement: Compare auxiliary space, retained memory, recomputation, and latency explicitly.
misconceptions: Using less memory automatically makes an algorithm better.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare auxiliary space, retained memory, recomputation, and latency explicitly.

competencies.statement: Benchmark geometric input sizes and compare measurements with the analytical model.
misconceptions: A benchmark can replace asymptotic analysis.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Benchmark geometric input sizes and compare measurements with the analytical model.

competencies.statement: Explain indexing and cache-local traversal from contiguous logical storage.
misconceptions: Python lists store full arbitrary objects inline.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain indexing and cache-local traversal from contiguous logical storage.

competencies.statement: Trace capacity growth and amortized append behavior through resize events.
misconceptions: Every append has constant worst-case cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace capacity growth and amortized append behavior through resize events.

competencies.statement: Compare indexed access, search, middle insertion, deletion, slicing, and copying costs.
misconceptions: All list operations are constant time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare indexed access, search, middle insertion, deletion, slicing, and copying costs.

competencies.statement: Maintain explicit two-pointer invariants for partition and interval problems.
misconceptions: Two pointers work only on sorted numeric arrays.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain explicit two-pointer invariants for partition and interval problems.

competencies.statement: Precompute prefix information to answer repeated range queries with validated boundaries.
misconceptions: Prefix structures remove all update cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Precompute prefix information to answer repeated range queries with validated boundaries.

competencies.statement: Trace singly and doubly linked node references without losing head, tail, or reachability.
misconceptions: Variables holding nodes contain copied node values.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace singly and doubly linked node references without losing head, tail, or reachability.

competencies.statement: Insert and delete at boundaries while preserving connectivity and size invariants.
misconceptions: Relinking one neighboring node is sufficient for every deletion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Insert and delete at boundaries while preserving connectivity and size invariants.

competencies.statement: Implement traversal with termination evidence and malformed-cycle protection.
misconceptions: Following next eventually reaches None in every linked structure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement traversal with termination evidence and malformed-cycle protection.

competencies.statement: Use fast and slow pointers for cycle, midpoint, and phase-offset reasoning.
misconceptions: Fast and slow pointers reveal cycle entry immediately.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use fast and slow pointers for cycle, midpoint, and phase-offset reasoning.

competencies.statement: Choose contiguous or linked storage from measured access, mutation, allocation, and locality needs.
misconceptions: Linked lists are always faster for insertion.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose contiguous or linked storage from measured access, mutation, allocation, and locality needs.

competencies.statement: Define stack, queue, and deque behavior independently from implementation details.
misconceptions: An abstract data type is a specific class layout.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define stack, queue, and deque behavior independently from implementation details.

competencies.statement: Apply LIFO state to parsing, undo, traversal, and nested-delimiter validation.
misconceptions: Recursion never uses stack-like state.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply LIFO state to parsing, undo, traversal, and nested-delimiter validation.

competencies.statement: Apply FIFO ordering with bounded capacity and explicit overload behavior.
misconceptions: A Python list front deletion is an efficient queue.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply FIFO ordering with bounded capacity and explicit overload behavior.

competencies.statement: Maintain a monotonic deque invariant for window extrema without stale entries.
misconceptions: Sorting each window has equivalent scaling.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a monotonic deque invariant for window extrema without stale entries.

competencies.statement: Test underflow, overflow, wraparound, duplicates, and long operation sequences.
misconceptions: Testing push then pop proves an ADT implementation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test underflow, overflow, wraparound, duplicates, and long operation sequences.

competencies.statement: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
misconceptions: Different values must always have different hashes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.

competencies.statement: Trace separate chaining and open addressing through collision and deletion cases.
misconceptions: A collision means the hash table is incorrect.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace separate chaining and open addressing through collision and deletion cases.

competencies.statement: Explain how load factor and resizing influence expected cost and memory.
misconceptions: Hash lookup is constant time under every input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain how load factor and resizing influence expected cost and memory.

competencies.statement: Use dictionaries, sets, counters, and default mappings according to semantic need.
misconceptions: A set is merely a list without duplicates.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use dictionaries, sets, counters, and default mappings according to semantic need.

competencies.statement: Test collision stress and avoid claims based only on average benign keys.
misconceptions: Random-looking keys prevent all worst cases.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test collision stress and avoid claims based only on average benign keys.

competencies.statement: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.
misconceptions: A recursive function only needs a base case somewhere.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.

competencies.statement: Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.
misconceptions: Recursive calls run in parallel by default.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.

competencies.statement: Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.
misconceptions: Every halving recurrence is logarithmic overall.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.

competencies.statement: Design balanced subproblems and prove the combine step preserves correctness.
misconceptions: Splitting a problem automatically improves runtime.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design balanced subproblems and prove the combine step preserves correctness.

competencies.statement: Choose recursion, explicit stack, or iteration from depth, clarity, and resource constraints.
misconceptions: Recursion is always less efficient than iteration.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose recursion, explicit stack, or iteration from depth, clarity, and resource constraints.

competencies.statement: Define total, partial, and key-based orderings including tie and missing-value policy.
misconceptions: A comparison function may return inconsistent answers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define total, partial, and key-based orderings including tie and missing-value policy.

competencies.statement: Trace insertion and selection sorting invariants and identify useful input regimes.
misconceptions: Quadratic algorithms have no legitimate use.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace insertion and selection sorting invariants and identify useful input regimes.

competencies.statement: Implement stable merge sort and account for auxiliary space and copying.
misconceptions: Merge sort always operates in place.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement stable merge sort and account for auxiliary space and copying.

competencies.statement: Trace partition invariants and pivot effects across duplicate and ordered inputs.
misconceptions: Quicksort is guaranteed n log n.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace partition invariants and pivot effects across duplicate and ordered inputs.

competencies.statement: Use Python stable key sorting and multi-pass ordering without comparator mistakes.
misconceptions: Reverse sorting reverses every tie relationship.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Python stable key sorting and multi-pass ordering without comparator mistakes.

competencies.statement: Maintain a precise candidate interval and prove each binary-search update shrinks it.
misconceptions: Binary search only needs a midpoint formula.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain a precise candidate interval and prove each binary-search update shrinks it.

competencies.statement: Find first true, last false, lower bound, and upper bound under duplicate values.
misconceptions: Finding any equal item solves every boundary query.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find first true, last false, lower bound, and upper bound under duplicate values.

competencies.statement: Binary-search an answer space only after proving a monotone feasibility predicate.
misconceptions: Any numeric answer can be binary searched.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Binary-search an answer space only after proving a monotone feasibility predicate.

competencies.statement: Trace partition-based selection and distinguish expected from worst-case cost.
misconceptions: Selecting the kth item requires fully sorting input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace partition-based selection and distinguish expected from worst-case cost.

competencies.statement: Generate empty, singleton, duplicate, extreme, and impossible-target cases.
misconceptions: One successful middle target catches off-by-one errors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate empty, singleton, duplicate, extreme, and impossible-target cases.

competencies.statement: Distinguish root, edge, parent, child, ancestor, depth, height, leaf, and subtree.
misconceptions: Depth and height are interchangeable for every node.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish root, edge, parent, child, ancestor, depth, height, leaf, and subtree.

competencies.statement: Implement preorder, inorder, postorder, and level-order traversal iteratively and recursively.
misconceptions: Inorder traversal sorts every kind of tree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement preorder, inorder, postorder, and level-order traversal iteratively and recursively.

competencies.statement: Preserve a documented duplicate policy and global ordering invariant during updates.
misconceptions: Checking each node against its children proves a BST valid.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Preserve a documented duplicate policy and global ordering invariant during updates.

competencies.statement: Implement search, insert, successor, and deletion across zero, one, and two-child cases.
misconceptions: Deleting a two-child node means dropping its subtree.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement search, insert, successor, and deletion across zero, one, and two-child cases.

competencies.statement: Measure how insertion order changes height and operation cost.
misconceptions: A binary search tree is automatically balanced.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure how insertion order changes height and operation cost.

competencies.statement: Relate complete-tree shape to array indices and parent-child heap order.
misconceptions: A heap keeps every element globally sorted.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate complete-tree shape to array indices and parent-child heap order.

competencies.statement: Trace sift-up, sift-down, push, pop, replace, and heapify costs.
misconceptions: Building a heap by heapify is n log n.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace sift-up, sift-down, push, pop, replace, and heapify costs.

competencies.statement: Add stable tie breakers without comparing unrelated payload objects.
misconceptions: Equal priorities can always compare their payloads safely.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Add stable tie breakers without comparing unrelated payload objects.

competencies.statement: Handle updated or removed priorities with entry finders and stale-entry cleanup.
misconceptions: A standard heap supports arbitrary update in constant time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle updated or removed priorities with entry finders and stale-entry cleanup.

competencies.statement: Use bounded heaps for top-k, streaming selection, and multiway merge problems.
misconceptions: Top-k always requires storing and sorting every item.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use bounded heaps for top-k, streaming selection, and multiway merge problems.

competencies.statement: Represent shared prefixes, terminal markers, and child transitions without confusing words and prefixes.
misconceptions: Every trie node represents a complete key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Represent shared prefixes, terminal markers, and child transitions without confusing words and prefixes.

competencies.statement: Implement insert, exact lookup, prefix lookup, delete, and bounded suggestions.
misconceptions: Removing a word always removes its full path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Implement insert, exact lookup, prefix lookup, delete, and bounded suggestions.

competencies.statement: Define Unicode normalization and case policy before indexing or comparison.
misconceptions: Lowercasing alone gives universal text equivalence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define Unicode normalization and case policy before indexing or comparison.

competencies.statement: Analyze prefix operations by key length and output size rather than record count alone.
misconceptions: Trie lookup is constant time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze prefix operations by key length and output size rather than record count alone.

competencies.statement: Trace brute-force substring matching and construct its worst-case repeated-prefix input.
misconceptions: Nested scanning always examines every pair of characters.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace brute-force substring matching and construct its worst-case repeated-prefix input.

competencies.statement: Define vertices, edges, direction, weights, self-loops, and parallel-edge policy from the domain.
misconceptions: Every relationship should be an undirected simple edge.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define vertices, edges, direction, weights, self-loops, and parallel-edge policy from the domain.

competencies.statement: Choose adjacency lists, matrices, edge lists, or implicit neighbors from density and operations.
misconceptions: Adjacency matrices are always faster because indexing is constant.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose adjacency lists, matrices, edge lists, or implicit neighbors from density and operations.

competencies.statement: Maintain BFS frontier and discovery invariants for unweighted shortest paths and layers.
misconceptions: BFS uses a stack and explores depth first.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain BFS frontier and discovery invariants for unweighted shortest paths and layers.

competencies.statement: Maintain DFS color or visited state for components, cycles, and traversal structure.
misconceptions: Marking visited after recursion is always safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain DFS color or visited state for components, cycles, and traversal structure.

competencies.statement: Analyze traversal over reachable vertices and represented edges with representation costs.
misconceptions: Graph traversal is always quadratic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze traversal over reachable vertices and represented edges with representation costs.

competencies.statement: Find connected components and explain what connectivity means for the modeled domain.
misconceptions: A disconnected graph necessarily indicates bad data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Find connected components and explain what connectivity means for the modeled domain.

competencies.statement: Detect undirected and directed cycles with the correct parent or active-path state.
misconceptions: Any visited neighbor proves a directed cycle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect undirected and directed cycles with the correct parent or active-path state.

competencies.statement: Produce and validate a topological order or return concrete cycle evidence.
misconceptions: A DAG has exactly one topological order.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce and validate a topological order or return concrete cycle evidence.

competencies.statement: Reconstruct an actual path from predecessor evidence instead of reporting reachability alone.
misconceptions: Visited state is enough to recover every path.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconstruct an actual path from predecessor evidence instead of reporting reachability alone.

competencies.statement: Use multiple initial frontier nodes for nearest-source and propagation problems.
misconceptions: Running separate BFS from every source has the same cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use multiple initial frontier nodes for nearest-source and propagation problems.

competencies.statement: Write executable representation invariants that fail near the corrupting operation.
misconceptions: Invariant checks belong only in final tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write executable representation invariants that fail near the corrupting operation.

competencies.statement: Compare custom operations against a simpler trusted reference model.
misconceptions: Using a library oracle makes the custom implementation pointless.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Compare custom operations against a simpler trusted reference model.

competencies.statement: Generate reproducible operation sequences and retain seeds for failures.
misconceptions: Randomized tests cannot be debugged.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate reproducible operation sequences and retain seeds for failures.

competencies.statement: Control setup, warmup, repetition, input distribution, and measured operation.
misconceptions: Timing an entire script isolates algorithm cost.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Control setup, warmup, repetition, input distribution, and measured operation.

competencies.statement: Require correctness equivalence and measured stakeholder benefit before keeping an optimization.
misconceptions: Lower Big O guarantees faster production behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require correctness equivalence and measured stakeholder benefit before keeping an optimization.

competencies.statement: Translate operation frequency, scale, latency, memory, and consistency needs into structure criteria.
misconceptions: Choose structures from their most famous operation only.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Translate operation frequency, scale, latency, memory, and consistency needs into structure criteria.

competencies.statement: Combine structures while documenting duplicated state and synchronization invariants.
misconceptions: Combining fast structures preserves each operation cost automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine structures while documenting duplicated state and synchronization invariants.

competencies.statement: Present loop invariants, induction, or contradiction arguments appropriate to each component.
misconceptions: Informal intuition is enough for high-impact correctness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Present loop invariants, induction, or contradiction arguments appropriate to each component.

competencies.statement: Test realistic, boundary, and adversarial workloads against analytical predictions.
misconceptions: Average sample data represents adversarial production input.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Test realistic, boundary, and adversarial workloads against analytical predictions.

competencies.statement: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.
misconceptions: Fast benchmark results alone justify the design.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend alternatives, limitations, failure recovery, and measured tradeoffs to reviewers.

credential.title: Data Structures and Algorithms in Python Certificate
